indentation-and-blocks.label: Indentation and Blocks
indentation-and-blocks.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

indentation-quiz-1.title: Fix the Indentation
indentation-quiz-1.prompt: Your task is to correct the indentation in the following code snippet:
indentation-quiz-1.hint: Break the task into the main query or code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply fix the indentation in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the table, columns, and condition or operation before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the statement one piece at a time: select the right fields, then add the condition or operation that matches the prompt.

indentation-quiz-2.title: Create an Indented Block
indentation-quiz-2.prompt: Write a Python function that checks if a number is even or odd and prints the result. Ensure proper indentation.
indentation-quiz-2.hint: Break the task into the main query or code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply create an indented block in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the table, columns, and condition or operation before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the statement one piece at a time: select the right fields, then add the condition or operation that matches the prompt.

indentation-quiz-3.title: What does indentation control in Python?
indentation-quiz-3.prompt: What is the primary purpose of indentation in Python?
indentation-quiz-3.hint: Think about how Python interprets your code.

help.concept: Indentation in Python is crucial as it defines the blocks of code that belong together, such as those under an if statement.
help.hint_2: Consider how other programming languages handle code blocks.

options.a: To make the code look nice
options.b: To define the structure of the code

indentation-quiz-4.title: Identify Indentation Errors
indentation-quiz-4.prompt: Select all the code snippets that contain indentation errors.

help.concept: Indentation errors occur when the code blocks are not properly aligned.
help.hint_1: Check for lines that should be indented but aren't.
help.hint_2: Remember that all lines in a block must have the same indentation level.

options.a:
if x > 5:
print('x is greater than 5')

indentation-quiz-5.title: Complete the Indentation Rule
indentation-quiz-5.prompt: Choose the best value for the first missing blank in the statement.
indentation-quiz-5.hint: Focus on the missing concept or keyword.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind complete the indentation rule.
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

indentation-basics.title: Understanding Indentation
indentation-basics.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of your code. For example:

```python
if condition:
    # This block is executed if the condition is true
    print('Condition is true')
else:
    # This block is executed if the condition is false
    print('Condition is false')
```

In this example, the indented lines under `if` and `else` are part of their respective blocks. If you forget to indent, Python will raise an `IndentationError`. 

**Your turn:** Try writing your own `if` statement with an `else` block!

Line by line: read the first statement to see which value is stored or checked, then follow each conditional branch in order to see which path prints the final result.

indentation-error.title: Common Indentation Errors
indentation-error.bodyMarkdown:
Indentation errors can be frustrating. For instance, if you mix tabs and spaces, Python will not know how to interpret your code. Here's an example of a common mistake:

```python
if condition:
print('This will cause an error')
```

In this case, the print statement should be indented. Always use either spaces or tabs consistently throughout your code.

**Try this:** Fix the indentation in the example above.

Line by line: read the first statement to see which value is stored or checked, then follow each conditional branch in order to see which path prints the final result.
